timeline_html:
Summary. Lhean’s hair changes began well over a year ago and have fluctuated in waves since then. She’s recently begun noticing new baby hairs, suggesting that her scalp is starting to respond positively. 🌱

Your hair-loss triggers.

  • Chronic stress from ongoing sleep deprivation and shift work (ongoing; started before Feb 2025)
  • Hormonal imbalance related to diagnosed thyroid condition (ongoing; started before Feb 2025)
  • Low iron history (resolved; date not provided)

Timeline forecast. Because her shedding began more than a year ago, the worst period likely occurred around spring–summer 2025, with the scalp now in its recovery phase. Stabilization should be well underway by early 2026, and visible baby hairs typically appear once shedding slows — which she’s already seeing. Dominant driver: chronic stress and disrupted sleep patterns, as these remain ongoing and can extend recovery even after the initial hormonal and iron-related factors have improved.

Where you are now. You’re in the early regrowth phase — shedding should be milder, and short new hairs are a positive sign that follicles are reactivating. Keep supporting rest and nutrition so this progress continues steadily. 🌱
